title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
Term Insurance With Return Of Premium
Term insurance is a pure form of life insurance. It is essentially a type of life insurance policy that provides the nominees or beneficiaries a death benefit in the event of the policyholder’s demise. In exchange for the life insurance coverage, the policyholder is required to make regular payments known as "premiums" to the insurance company.
Unlike other types of life insurance plans, the pay-out in term insurance is only made in the case of the policyholder’s death during the policy term and not upon the term policy’s maturity if the holder survives the tenure. It is primarily for this reason that the premiums charged for term insurance might be lower than the other types of life insurance.
However, now you can also buy term insurance with return of premium that provides a return of premium on maturity, which means you will receive the money you have paid in your term insurance policy if you live through the term of the policy.
Term Plan With Return Of Premium
A term insurance plan with a return of premium option is one which not only provides insurance protection over the policy tenure but also provides a maturity benefit. If the life insured survives the policy tenure and the plan matures, the premiums are refunded under term insurance with return of premium feature.
Term insurance with return of premium comes under two alternatives. Have a look –
Individual policy
There are some individual term insurance plans which come with return of premium feature by default.
Add-On with a normal policy
Some term insurance plans offer the return of premium benefit as an added option. You can choose to add the option to the basic term insurance policy and enjoy premium refunds on maturity. For instance, Bajaj Allianz Life Smart Protection Goal is a term plan with the option to choose the return of premium benefit on maturity.
How Does A Term Insurance Plan With Return Of Premium Work?
Under the return of premium term life insurance plan, you choose the sum assured, policy tenure, premium payment tenure and frequency, just like a regular term plan. Based on these details, your age and other risk factors, the premium is calculated. You pay the premium over the chosen premium payment tenure and in the chosen frequency.
If the life insured passes away during the policy tenure, the death benefit is paid. On the other hand, if the insured survives the policy tenure, the premiums paid over the policy tenure are refunded to the policyholder.
Who Can Buy Term Insurance With Return Of Premium?
Individuals meeting the eligibility parameters specified by the insurance company can buy a term life insurance plan with return of a premium option. The plan is suitable for individuals looking for a maturity benefit from their term insurance plan and those who may not mind paying an additional nominal premium for the same.
How Is It Different From A Regular Term Insurance Plan?
Term insurance plan with return of premium is different from a regular term plan. Here are some aspects of how –
Reasons You Should Choose A Term Plan With A Return Of Premium Option?
If you are looking for optimal financial protection against the risk of premature demise and also don’t want to lose out on the premiums on maturity, a term insurance plan with return of premium makes sense.
Plus, some of the benefits of a term insurance plan with return of premium, just like the traditional term plans are as follows –
Financial protection
Being a term plan, the return of the premium plan gives financial protection against the risk of premature demise. If the breadwinner buys the policy, he ensures the financial protection of his family in his absence. The family receives a death benefit if the insured passes away during the policy tenure.
Peace of mind
By ensuring financial security for your family, you can enjoy peace of mind. You are assured that in your absence your family is financially taken care of so that they can meet their goals. Moreover, if you have any liabilities, the death benefit can pay them off so that the financial burden doesn’t fall on the family.
Tax benefits
Like other life insurance plans, tax benefits are also available under term insurance plan with return of premium. The premium paid is allowed as a deduction under Section 80C up to Rs.1.5 lakhs only under the old Regime of Tax1. The death benefit received is tax-free2. The maturity benefit is also tax-free subject to specified terms and conditions2.Any gain from term insurance policies with return of premium option issued on or after 1 April 2023 with premium more than Rs. 5 lakhs is taxable in the hands of recipient. Death benefit continues to be tax-free in the hands of recipient. Tax benefits are subject to changes in tax laws and terms and conditions.
Helps in meeting financial goals
If the plan matures and the premiums are refunded, you get a financial corpus which can be used to fulfil your financial goals.
Conclusion
Term insurance plans with a return of premium provide protection during the tenure which provides financial security. Plus, the maturity benefit gives back the premiums you paid for securing the coverage. So, you get insured and you don’t lose out on the premiums too.
So, if you want a maturity benefit from your term insurance, choose the return of premium plan and get a lump sum benefit on maturity.
FAQs
Can you get your money back from term insurance on maturity?
Traditional term insurance plans do not offer any maturity benefit. They are meant to cover premature demise and pay a benefit only if the insured passes away during the policy tenure. However, if you choose the terms plans with return of premium feature, you can get your money back when the plan matures. Under such plans, the premiums are refunded if the insured survives the policy tenure.
What are some of the factors which may make a term plan with a return of premium a suitable plan?
Some factors that make term insurance with return of premium a suitable option include a maturity benefit, insurance coverage during the policy tenure, tax benefits as well as financial security.
Which companies offer a term insurance plan with a return of premium option?
Many companies offer the term insurance plan with a return of premium option. You can check the availability of such plans across insurers. Bajaj Allianz also offers the return of premium option with its Smart Protection Goal policy wherein you can choose the option and receive the premium refund on maturity.
